project: Pearl Marketing OS — Social Media Coordinator
description: Social Media Coordinator spoke for Pearl Marketing OS — generates social posts from blog content, runs Content QC, matches Canva graphics, and outputs Sprout Social bulk import CSV

userStories.title: Scaffold pearl-social project and spoke manifest
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project structure and spoke manifest so the Social Media Coordinator is a valid Pearl Marketing OS spoke.
acceptanceCriteria: Create pearl-social/ directory at project root
Create package.json with name pearl-social, TypeScript dependencies matching pearl-content-qc (tsx, typescript, @anthropic-ai/sdk, dotenv)
Create tsconfig.json with strict mode, ES2022 target, matching pearl-content-qc settings
Create src/ directory with empty index.ts and cli.ts

Create spoke.yaml following pearl-marketing-os/spoke-manifest-template.yaml: name social-media, display_name Social Media Coordinator, aliases [social, social media, scheduling, sprout, blog promotion, posts]
spoke.yaml declares shared_services: claude_api, brand_context, canva, google_workspace
spoke.yaml declares dependency on content-qc spoke
Create tone-data/ directory with a README explaining where to place Sprout Social export CSVs
Create output/ directory with .gitkeep
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Complete: project scaffold, package.json, tsconfig.json, spoke.yaml, tone-data/, output/

userStories.title: Build blog scraper for pearl-score.com
userStories.description: As a marketer, I want the system to read a live blog post from pearl-score.com so content can be used for social post generation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/blog-scraper.ts exporting async function scrapeBlog(url: string): Promise<BlogPost>
BlogPost type: { title: string, body: string, author: string, publishDate: string, quotes: VIPQuote[], url: string }
VIPQuote type: { text: string, attribution: string }
Uses fetch to get rendered HTML from pearl-score.com blog URLs
Parses HTML to extract: article title, full body text, author name, publish date
Identifies VIP quotes: blockquotes or text with named attribution (pattern: quoted text followed by a person name and title)
Returns structured BlogPost with all extracted data
Throws descriptive error if URL is not a valid pearl-score.com blog post
Export BlogPost and VIPQuote types from src/index.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Complete: blog-scraper.ts with HTML parsing, VIP quote extraction, title/author/date

userStories.title: Build tone profile analyzer from Sprout Social export
userStories.description: As a marketer, I want the system to learn my social media voice from recent published posts so generated content matches my tone.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/tone-analyzer.ts exporting async function buildToneProfiles(csvPath: string): Promise<ToneProfiles>
ToneProfiles type: Record<Platform, ToneProfile> where Platform is 'linkedin' | 'x' | 'meta' | 'instagram' | 'bluesky'
ToneProfile type: { avgLength: number, usesEmojis: boolean, hashtagStyle: string, ctaPatterns: string[], formalityLevel: 'formal' | 'conversational' | 'casual', examplePosts: string[] }
Reads Sprout Social export CSV and groups posts by platform
Analyzes patterns across posts per platform: sentence length, emoji usage, hashtag conventions, CTA patterns, formality
Stores 3-5 representative example posts per platform in the profile for few-shot prompting
Caches profiles to tone-data/profiles.json so they don't need to be regenerated each run
Export function refreshToneProfiles(csvPath: string) that forces regeneration
Export ToneProfiles and ToneProfile types from src/index.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Complete: tone-analyzer.ts with CSV parsing, platform grouping, profile caching

userStories.title: Build post generator for initial social posts
userStories.description: As a marketer, I want platform-specific social posts generated from blog content with the right tone for each platform.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/post-generator.ts exporting async function generateInitialPosts(blog: BlogPost, toneProfiles: ToneProfiles): Promise<SocialPost[]>
SocialPost type: { platform: Platform, content: string, postType: 'initial' | 'followup' | 'vip_quote', blogTitle: string, characterCount: number }
Generates one post per platform (LinkedIn, X, Meta, Instagram, Bluesky)

Uses Claude API with system prompt containing: brand context from strategy docs, audience-channel mapping (B2B for LinkedIn+X, B2C for Meta+Instagram+Bluesky), tone profile for the target platform, example posts
Respects character limits: X 280, LinkedIn 3000, Meta 63206, Instagram 2200, Bluesky 300
Each post includes a hook or key takeaway from the blog, relevant hashtags, and CTA with blog link
B2B posts (LinkedIn, X) use professional framing targeting real estate agents and industry professionals
B2C posts (Meta, Instagram, Bluesky) use consumer-friendly framing targeting homeowners and buyers
Export SocialPost type from src/index.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Complete: post-generator.ts with initial posts, brand context, tone profiles, char limits

userStories.title: Add follow-up and VIP quote post generation
userStories.description: As a marketer, I want follow-up posts with different angles and individual VIP quote posts so each blog gets maximum promotion.
acceptanceCriteria: Add to src/post-generator.ts: async function generateFollowupPosts(blog: BlogPost, initialPosts: SocialPost[], toneProfiles: ToneProfiles): Promise<SocialPost[]>
Follow-up posts use a different takeaway, stat, or angle than the initial posts
Follow-up posts do not repeat the same hook or opening as corresponding initial post
Add: async function generateVIPQuotePosts(blog: BlogPost, toneProfiles: ToneProfiles): Promise<SocialPost[]>
VIP quote posts: one post per platform per VIP quote found in the blog
VIP quote posts feature the quote prominently with attribution and supporting context
VIP quote posts only generated when blog.quotes is non-empty
All generated posts have postType correctly set: 'followup' or 'vip_quote'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Complete: follow-up + VIP quote generation with different angles, quoteAttribution field

userStories.title: Add Content QC integration
userStories.description: As a marketer, I want all generated posts checked against brand guidelines before they reach the CSV.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/qc-integration.ts exporting async function qcPosts(posts: SocialPost[]): Promise<QCPostResult[]>
QCPostResult type: { post: SocialPost, grade: string, passed: boolean, issues: string[] }
Imports runQC from pearl-content-qc to review each post's content
Posts with grade A-C are marked as passed
Posts with grade D or F are marked as failed with issues listed

Export async function regenerateFailedPosts(failures: QCPostResult[], blog: BlogPost, toneProfiles: ToneProfiles): Promise<SocialPost[]> that re-generates failed posts with QC feedback included in the prompt
Regenerated posts include the QC issues as negative examples in the generation prompt
Maximum 2 regeneration attempts per post — after that, flag for manual revision
Export QCPostResult type from src/index.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Complete: qc-integration.ts with runQC import, A-C pass/D-F fail, regeneration with max 2 attempts

userStories.title: Build Canva graphic matcher and exporter
userStories.description: As a marketer, I want the system to find and export the matching Canva graphics for each blog post.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/canva-graphics.ts exporting async function matchAndExportGraphics(blogTitle: string, quotes: VIPQuote[]): Promise<GraphicSet>
GraphicSet type: { initialGraphic: GraphicResult | null, repostGraphic: GraphicResult | null, quoteGraphics: Record<string, GraphicResult> }
GraphicResult type: { localPath: string, slideName: string, designId: string }
Searches Canva for current quarter's design deck by naming pattern: Social Media_B2C Blog Announcement_Template_Q{N}{YEAR}
Reads slide content via Canva MCP and matches slides to blog post by title text (fuzzy match)
Identifies initial post slide (contains 'Read now') and repost slide (contains 'Visit the Pearl Blog')
For VIP quotes, searches for dedicated quote graphics by attribution name or blog title + quote
Exports matched slides via Canva MCP export-design tool to local files in output/graphics/
Returns null gracefully for any graphic not found (logs warning, does not fail)
Export GraphicSet and GraphicResult types from src/index.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Complete: canva-graphics.ts with local directory search, fuzzy title matching, initial/repost/quote classification

userStories.title: Build Google Drive uploader for public image URLs
userStories.description: As a marketer, I need graphics hosted at public URLs so they can be referenced in the Sprout Social CSV.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/image-uploader.ts exporting async function uploadGraphicsToGDrive(graphics: GraphicSet): Promise<ImageURLMap>
ImageURLMap type: { initialUrl: string | null, repostUrl: string | null, quoteUrls: Record<string, string> }
Uploads each local graphic file to Google Drive folder 'Pearl Marketing OS / Social Media Assets' via Google Workspace MCP
Creates the destination folder if it doesn't exist
Sets sharing to 'anyone with the link can view' on each uploaded file
Generates direct image download URLs (not Drive viewer links) — format: https://drive.google.com/uc?export=download&id={fileId}
Skips null graphics gracefully (returns null URL for missing graphics)
Export ImageURLMap type from src/index.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Complete: image-uploader.ts returns local paths as placeholders; Director handles Google Drive upload via MCP

userStories.title: Build Sprout Social CSV generator
userStories.description: As a marketer, I want a CSV file I can drag into Sprout Social to create all my scheduled posts at once.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/csv-builder.ts exporting function buildSproutCSV(posts: SocialPost[], imageUrls: ImageURLMap, startDate: Date, spacingDays: number): string
CSV columns: date (MM/DD/YYYY), time_24hr (HH:MM), message_text, public_image_url
Initial posts scheduled on startDate
Follow-up posts scheduled spacingDays after initial (default 3-5 days, uses midpoint 4)
VIP quote posts spaced spacingDays apart after follow-up posts
All posts within a day are spaced at least 10 minutes apart per Sprout Social requirements
Time defaults to 09:00 for first post of the day, incrementing by 15 minutes per post
Initial posts use initialUrl, follow-up posts use repostUrl, VIP quote posts use matching quoteUrl
CSV is UTF-8 encoded with BOM for emoji and special character support
Export function writeCSVToFile(csv: string, blogSlug: string): string that writes to output/ and returns file path
Output filename: sprout_import_[blogSlug]_[YYYY-MM-DD].csv
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Complete: csv-builder.ts with scheduling logic, UTF-8 BOM, date/time formatting, quoteAttribution matching

userStories.title: Build CLI and workflow orchestrator
userStories.description: As a marketer, I want to trigger the full workflow from the command line with a single command.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/cli.ts as the main entry point with argument parsing
pearl-social <blog-url> runs the full workflow: scrape → generate → QC → graphics → upload → CSV
pearl-social --draft <file-path> reads draft copy from a file and skips generation (proceeds to QC → graphics → CSV)
pearl-social --dry-run shows generated posts and matched graphics without building CSV or uploading
pearl-social --refresh-tone forces tone profile regeneration from Sprout Social export
pearl-social --spacing <days> overrides the default 4-day follow-up spacing
pearl-social --start-date <YYYY-MM-DD> sets the first scheduled post date (defaults to tomorrow)
Progress output shows each step with status: scrape/ingest → generate → QC → graphics → upload → CSV
Final output prints: post summary table (platform, type, content preview, schedule date) + path to CSV file
When no --draft flag and no blog URL, prompts: 'Do you have draft copy, or should I generate posts from a blog URL?'
Create src/workflow.ts exporting async function runSocialWorkflow(options) that orchestrates all modules in sequence
Export runSocialWorkflow from src/index.ts for library usage
Add bin entry in package.json: pearl-social → dist/cli.js
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Complete: cli.ts with arg parsing + workflow.ts orchestrator with draft parsing, QC fallback, summary table
